title: Step Count Print: A Physical Activity-Based Biometric Identifier for User Identification and Authentication
venue: IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science

method.input_representation: Step Count Print (SCP) consisting of cadence distribution, average step count sequence, start/end times, and exercise frequency.
method.training_strategy: Leave-one-out cross-validation for hyper-parameters; 70% training, 15% validation, and 15% testing split.

evaluation_protocol: User identification (1:N) and user authentication (1:1) scenarios using k-NN for feature selection and various ML classifiers for evaluation.

reported_limitations: Lack of permanence (requires regular template updates)
Difficulty distinguishing users with sedentary lifestyles
Performance decreases as the number of users in the system increases

key_contribution: Introduction of Step Count Print (SCP) as a novel behavioral biometric derived from coarse-grained minute-level data
Demonstration of feasibility for both user identification and authentication using real-world longitudinal data
main_weakness: Significantly lower identification rates compared to physiological biometrics
Lack of permanence of behavioral patterns over time
insights.notes_for_review: The paper focuses on coarse-grained data (minute-level) rather than the high-frequency raw sensor data typical in many wearable biometric studies.
